_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**MEPC review of CII/EEXI effectiveness — mandated by 1 January 2026**

- **Issuing body:** IMO Marine Environment Protection Committee (MEPC)
- **Expected date:** No later than 1 January 2026, per the adopted amendment. MEPC sessions in the 2025–2026 window (MEPC 82, MEPC 83) are the likely vehicles. Exact session dates for MEPC 82 and MEPC 83 are not confirmed in the available source material and should be checked on the IMO meeting schedule.
- **Impact:** The review may produce amendments to CII reduction factors (tightening or relaxing annual improvement requirements), revised rating thresholds, expanded or contracted vessel scope, or new consequences for D/E-rated vessels. Any amendment is material to carrier procurement and tender design.
- **What the workspace should expect to decide:** Whether to revise its carrier panel exclusion criteria and client-facing sustainability commitments in response to amended thresholds.

*Source: IMO Press Briefing, IMO, 1 November 2022: "MEPC is to review the effectiveness of the implementation of the CII and EEXI requirements by 1 January 2026 at the latest and develop and adopt further amendments as required."*

**2023 Revised IMO GHG Strategy — adopted July 2023**

- **Issuing body:** IMO MEPC 80
- **Status:** Adopted 7 July 2023 via Resolution MEPC.377(80). The revised strategy strengthens the 2018 Initial Strategy, setting net-zero GHG emissions from international shipping "by or around 2050" and indicative checkpoints of at least 20% reduction by 2030 and 70% reduction by 2040 vs 2008.
- **Impact on CII:** The revised strategy is the policy framework within which CII reduction factors will be assessed and tightened. The mid-term measures under development (fuel levy, market-based mechanisms) may interact with or supersede CII as the primary instrument. The workspace should monitor MEPC outputs for any decision to adjust CII as mid-term measures advance.

*Source: Resolution MEPC.377(80), "2023 IMO Strategy on Reduction of GHG Emissions from Ships," IMO, adopted 7 July 2023. https://wwwcdn.imo.org/localresources/en/KnowledgeCentre/IndexofIMOResolutions/MEPCDocuments/MEPC.377(80).pdf*

*Source: IMO, "2023 IMO Strategy on Reduction of GHG Emissions from Ships," https://www.imo.org/en/ourwork/environment/pages/2023-imo-strategy-on-reduction-of-ghg-emissions-from-ships.aspx*
